steps.title: Identify the subject requiring a main verb
steps.content: ”Puerto Rico's museums” is a plural subject that needs a main verb to complete the independent clause.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: The base form inform is the correct present-tense verb form for this plural subject. The other three choices—the infinitive to inform and the participles having informed and informing—are non-finite forms that cannot function as main verbs. That matches choice C — C is correct.
